chunk_summary: This section discusses phenotype-based classification for managing undernutrition and overweight, emphasizing mortality risk prediction, treatment protocols, and specific guidelines for stunting, wasting, and obesity in children.
learning_objectives: Understand phenotype-based classification for undernutrition and overweight management.
Learn outpatient care protocols for stunting, wasting, and concurrent phenotypes like WaSt.
Identify guidelines for managing stunting with overweight or obesity in children.
Apply IAP obesity principles to reduce ultra-processed foods and improve nutrient density.
key_facts: Moderate acute malnutrition (MAM): weight-for-height between -2 and -3 SD or MUAC 115–125 mm (6–59 months) without edema.
Severe acute malnutrition (SAM): weight-for-height < -3 SD and/or MUAC <115 mm and/or nutritional edema.
Concurrent wasting plus stunting (WaSt) is a high-mortality phenotype requiring urgent management.
Overweight classification: weight-for-height > +2 SD under 5 years and BMI-for-age in ages 5–19 years.
Avoid indiscriminate extra calories for stunting with overweight; focus on nutrient density and protein sufficiency.
reference_quote: Classify overweight using weight-for-height > +2 SD under 5 years and BMI-for-age in ages 5–19 years. Avoid indiscriminate 'extra calories' or appetite stimulants; prescribe nutrient density, protein sufficiency, and micronutrient repletion while reducing sugar-sweetened beverages and ultra-processed foods.
document_key_topics: Double burden of malnutrition: stunting with overweight
Guidelines for obesity management in children
